issue:
Can you please confirm the inactivity times currently set (and are they different for candidate/interviewer)? 
Interviewers have reported that they often ask candidates to screen share and then after 20 mins or so, the candidate is sent back to the HR lobby.

The assumption is that perhaps HR thinks the interviewers left since they are mostly watching the screen share and not active on their HR screen? 
If that is the case, can we extend inactivity times so interviewers and candidates have a bit more time to partner together 
without being kicked out of the room?
subject: Candidate inactivity help
response: Currently, the inactivity times for candidates and interviewers are not explicitly detailed in the documentation. However, candidates are automatically pushed back to the lobby when all interviewers leave the interview. To address the issue of inactivity, you may want to consider enabling the virtual lobby, which allows interviewers to manage candidate presence more effectively. For further adjustments, please consult your company’s admin settings or reach out to your HR team for specific configurations.
justification: I replied because the inquiry pertains to interview settings and inactivity times, which can be managed through the company-level admin settings. The response is grounded in the retrieved snippets regarding interview settings and the virtual lobby.

subject: Help needed
response: If you are experiencing issues with the HackerRank platform, please submit a request by selecting **Support** in the footer of the HackerRank for Work platform or email us at support@hackerrank.com for assistance.
justification: I replied because the ticket describes a support issue that can be addressed through documented self-service options, directing the user to submit a request or email support.

issue:
Hello! I am trying to remove an interviewer from the platform. I am not seeing this as an option when I select the three dots next to their name. 
Can you let me know how to do this?
subject: How to Remove a User
response: To remove an interviewer from an interview template, go to **Interviews > Templates**, click the **More options (⋮)** menu next to the template, select **Share**, choose the user you want to remove, and then select **Remove User** from the drop-down menu. Finally, click **Confirm** in the confirmation dialog box.
justification: I replied because the request involves a documented self-service workflow for removing user access from an interview template, which is clearly outlined in the retrieved corpus.

issue: Hi, please pause our subscription. We have stopped all hiring efforts for now.
subject: Subscription pause
response: To pause your subscription, please follow these steps: Click on your profile icon in the top-right corner, select Settings, and navigate to the Billing section under Subscription. Then, click the Cancel Plan button, and you will see a prompt about the Pause Subscription feature. Confirm your selection to pause your subscription.
justification: I replied because the request to pause the subscription is a documented self-service workflow that the user can follow, as outlined in the retrieved snippets.

issue: Hello, I have completed an assessment, but my name is incorrect on the certificate. Can you please update it
subject: Certificate name update
response: You can update the name on your certificate by following these steps: 1. Open your certificate page. 2. In the **Full Name** field, enter the name you want to display. 3. Click **Regenerate Certificate**. 4. In the confirmation dialog, click **Update Name**. Note that you can only update your name once per account, and this change will apply to all your certificates.
justification: I replied because the user can self-service the name update on their certificate by following the documented steps provided in the certifications FAQs.

issue: one of my employee has left. I want to remove them from our hackerrank hiring account
subject: Employee leaving the company
response: To remove an employee from your HackerRank hiring account, you need to revoke their access to tests. Log in to your HackerRank for Work account, go to the Tests tab, open the test from which you want to revoke access, click Share, and then select Remove User next to their name.
justification: I replied because the request involves a documented workflow for revoking access to tests, which is a self-service action that can be performed by the account admin.
